Tests the behavior of the `arguments` object in a function.

# Purpose
This code defines a function `f_arg` that takes two parameters, `x` and `y`, and returns the `arguments` object. The `arguments` object is an array-like object that contains the values of the arguments passed to the function. The code includes an assertion to test the `f_arg` function by calling it with a single argument, `1`, and checking if the first element of the returned `arguments` object is equal to `1`. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on demonstrating the behavior of the `arguments` object in JavaScript functions.
